file.content:
enum FieldVisibility {
  VISIBLE
  RESTRICTED
  HIDDEN
}

enum FieldSearchability {
  SEARCHABLE
  RESTRICTED
  NOT_SEARCHABLE
}

enum Theme {
  CORE
  LOCATION
  ACADEMICS
  COST
  OUTCOMES
  RANKING
  ADMISSIONS
  ATHLETICS
  FIELDS_OF_STUDY
  CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT
  WEATHER
  PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT
  DEMOGRAPHICS
  REPUTATION
  SAFETY
  STUDENT_LIFE
  METADATA
  OTHER
}

# Shared fields on every search field type
interface SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# value is what is sent in filter payloads; displayName is shown to the user
type SearchFieldOption {
  value: String!
  displayName: String!
}

enum SearchFieldUnit {
  DOLLARS
  PERCENTAGE
  MILES
  HOURS
  MINUTES
}

# Full-text, substring, or exact-string text filter
type TextSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  minLength: Int
  maxLength: Int
  pattern: String
}

# Boolean toggle — checked sends value "1"; unchecked omits the filter entirely
type BinarySearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# Single- or multi-select from a fixed option list
type ChoiceSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  options: [SearchFieldOption!]!
  minSelections: Int!
  maxSelections: Int
}

# Single exact numeric value
type NumericalSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  min: Float
  max: Float
  step: Float
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  defaultValue: Float
}

# Numeric lower–upper range pair
type NumericalRangeSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  min: Float
  max: Float
  step: Float
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  defaultLower: Float
  defaultUpper: Float
  minSpan: Float
  maxSpan: Float
}

# Geo proximity / bounding-box filter
type GeoSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  defaultLatitude: Float
  defaultLongitude: Float
  maxRadiusMiles: Float!
}

type CipCode {
  code: String!
  description: String!
}

extend type Query {
  searchFields: [SearchField!]!
  searchOutputFields: [SearchOutputField!]!
  cipCodes(search: String!, limit: Int): [CipCode!]!
  cipCodesByCode(codes: [String!]!): [CipCode!]!
}

# Describes a field that appears in search result rows. Shares name/label/
# description/visibility/exportable with the corresponding SearchField where
# one exists; adds display-specific attributes (defaultVisible, sortable).
type SearchOutputField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  # defaultVisible: true = shown by default in the results grid;
  # false = available in the column picker but hidden by default.
  defaultVisible: Boolean!
  # sortable: true = the column header shows a sort control.
  sortable: Boolean!
  # unit: optional hint for how to format the displayed value.
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  # outputKey: dot-separated path into the data JSONB blob for extracting
  # the field's value from SearchInstitution.data. Null for fields that are
  # direct properties on SearchInstitution (name, city, state, etc.).
  outputKey: String
}

1→package resolver
     2→
     3→// This file contains hand-written helpers for the searchFields resolver.
     4→// Keep helpers here (not in search_fields.resolvers.go) so gqlgen does not
     5→// move them into a comment block on regeneration.
     6→
     7→import (
     8→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/graph/model"
     9→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// intPtr returns a pointer to an int literal.
    13→func intPtr(v int) *int { return &v }
    14→
    15→// strPtr returns a pointer to a string, or nil if the string is empty.
    16→func strPtr(s string) *string {
    17→	if s == "" {
    18→		return nil
    19→	}
    20→	return &s
    21→}
    22→
    23→// isBinaryEnum returns true if the options are exactly [{Value:"0"}, {Value:"1"}].
    24→func isBinaryEnum(opts []search.EnumOption) bool {
    25→	if len(opts) != 2 {
    26→		return false
    27→	}
    28→	return opts[0].Value == "0" && opts[1].Value == "1"
    29→}

   597→// Accepts "men-only", "women-only", or "coed" and translates to the appropriate
   598→// SQL against the existing data columns without any schema changes.
   599→// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

   615→func (f *GenderField) BuildSQL(rawFilter json.RawMessage, argStart int) (string, []any, error) {

   617→	if err := json.Unmarshal(rawFilter, &filter); err != nil {

   622→	womenRef := "data #>> '{nces,women_only}'"

   626→		expr := fmt.Sprintf("%s IS NOT NULL AND %s = $%d", menRef, menRef, argStart)

   628→	case "women-only":

   630→		return expr, []any{"1"}, nil
   631→	case "coed":
   632→		expr := fmt.Sprintf(
   633→			"(%s IS NULL OR %s = $%d) AND (%s IS NULL OR %s = $%d)",
   634→			menRef, menRef, argStart,
   635→			womenRef, womenRef, argStart+1,
   636→		)
   637→		return expr, []any{"0", "0"}, nil

input.content:
# Plan: Alpha-sort facet options (kapow-53vy)

## Context

Facet sidebar checkboxes and dropdowns render options in registration order (the order
they appear in `registry.go`). This is inconsistent and arbitrary for most fields.
The fix: sort alphabetically by default on the frontend, but let the API signal
"preserve my defined order" for fields with a meaningful sequence (urban→rural,
educational progression, climate zones, etc.).

---

## Enum facets inventory & ordering decision

| Field | Type | Options | Decision |
|---|---|---|---|
| `locale` | SingleEnum | 12 | **Ordered** — City:Large→Rural:Remote gradient |
| `region` | SingleEnum | 9 | **Ordered** — geographic grouping (NE→Outlying) |
| `highest_degree` | SingleEnum | 5 | **Ordered** — Certificate→Doctorate progression |
| `ncaa_division` | SingleEnum | 3 | **Ordered** — Division I→II→III |
| `koppen_climate` | SingleEnum | 5 | **Ordered** — Tropical→Dry→Temperate→Continental→Polar |
| `koppen_temperature` | SingleEnum | 9 | **Ordered** — Köppen sub-type scientific ordering |
| `koppen_precipitation` | SingleEnum | 3 | **Ordered** — scientific ordering |
| `state` | MultiColumnEnum | 50 | Alpha-sort (already alphabetical via `allStateOptions()`) |
| `institution_type` | SingleEnum | 3 | Alpha-sort |
| `coeducation_status` | GenderField | 3 | Alpha-sort |
| `application_platforms` | MultiEnum | 3 | Alpha-sort |

---

## Implementation

### Step 1 — API: add `OptionsOrdered` to `FieldDescriptor`

**File**: `api/internal/search/field.go`

Add field to `FieldDescriptor` struct (after `SelectionMode`):
```go
OptionsOrdered bool `json:"options_ordered,omitempty"`
```

### Step 2 — API: add `Ordered bool` to enum field structs

**File**: `api/internal/search/field.go`

Add `Ordered bool` to all four enum field structs:
- `SingleEnumField` (line ~570)
- `GenderField` (line ~601)
- `MultiColumnEnumField` (line ~650)
- `MultiEnumField` (line ~682)

Update each struct's `Descriptor()` method to propagate the flag:
```go
d.OptionsOrdered = f.Ordered
```

### Step 3 — API: GraphQL schema

**File**: `api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql`

Add `optionsOrdered` to `ChoiceSearchField`:
```graphql
# optionsOrdered: when true, options are in a meaningful defined order (e.g. urban→rural
# gradient, educational progression) and should be displayed as-is. When false,
# the client should sort options alphabetically.
optionsOrdered: Boolean!
```

### Step 4 — Regenerate API code

```bash
cd api && make api-generate
```

This updates `graph/generated/generated.go` and `graph/model/models_gen.go`.

### Step 5 — API: resolver helper

**File**: `api/graph/resolver/search_fields_helpers.go`

Update both `case search.KindSingleEnum` and `case search.KindMultiEnum` blocks to
populate `OptionsOrdered` from `d.OptionsOrdered`:
```go
return &model.ChoiceSearchField{
    ...
    Options:        enumOptionsToModel(d.Options),
    OptionsOrdered: d.OptionsOrdered,
    ...
}
```

### Step 6 — API: registry — mark ordered fields

**File**: `api/internal/search/registry.go`

Add `Ordered: true` to the 7 fields with meaningful ordering:
`locale`, `region`, `highest_degree`, `ncaa_division`, `koppen_climate`,
`koppen_temperature`, `koppen_precipitation`.

### Step 7 — Sync web schema

```bash
make web-schema-sync
```

Updates `web/schema.graphql` and `web/src/graphql-env.d.ts` with the new
`optionsOrdered` field.

### Step 8 — Frontend: update GraphQL query

**File**: `web/src/pages/Search.tsx`

Add `optionsOrdered` to the `ChoiceSearchField` fragment in `SearchFieldsQuery`:
```graphql
... on ChoiceSearchField {
  options {
    value
    displayName
  }
  optionsOrdered
  minSelections
  maxSelections
}
```

### Step 9 — Frontend: sort options in rendering

**File**: `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx`

The `MultiSelectCheckboxes` component (line ~52) receives `field` as a prop.
Add a sort step before rendering:

```tsx
const displayOptions = field.optionsOrdered
  ? field.options
  : [...field.options].sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName))
```

Apply the same sort for the single-select dropdown case (the `<select>` at line ~384).

Also pass `optionsOrdered` through `FilterControl` props to `MultiSelectCheckboxes` as needed.

### Step 10 — Tests

**API test** (`api/internal/search/search_test.go` or a new `field_test.go`):
- Verify that `SingleEnumField{Ordered: true}.Descriptor().OptionsOrdered == true`
- Verify that `SingleEnumField{Ordered: false}.Descriptor().OptionsOrdered == false`

**Frontend test** (`web/src/components/search/FilterControl.test.tsx`):
- Test that options render in alphabetical order when `optionsOrdered: false`
- Test that options preserve defined order when `optionsOrdered: true`

---

## Critical files

- `api/internal/search/field.go` — `FieldDescriptor`, `SingleEnumField`, `GenderField`, `MultiColumnEnumField`, `MultiEnumField`
- `api/internal/search/registry.go` — all field definitions
- `api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql` — `ChoiceSearchField` type
- `api/graph/resolver/search_fields_helpers.go` — `descriptorToSearchField()`
- `web/src/pages/Search.tsx` — `SearchFieldsQuery`
- `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx` — `MultiSelectCheckboxes` + dropdown

---

## Verification

```bash
# API quality gate (from worktree root)
make api-test-unit && make api-lint

# Frontend quality gate
cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test
```

**File**: `api/internal/search/field.go`

Add field to `FieldDescriptor` struct (after `SelectionMode`):

```go
OptionsOrdered bool `json:"options_ordered,omitempty"`
```

### Step 2 — API: add `Ordered bool` to enum field structs

**File**: `api/internal/search/field.go`

Add `Ordered bool` to all four enum field structs:
- `SingleEnumField` (line ~570)
- `GenderField` (line ~601)
- `MultiColumnEnumField` (line ~650)
- `MultiEnumField` (line ~682)

Update each struct's `Descriptor()` method to propagate the flag:
```go
d.OptionsOrdered = f.Ordered
```

### Step 3 — API: GraphQL schema

**File**: `api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql`

Add `optionsOrdered` to `ChoiceSearchField`:
```graphql
# optionsOrdered: when true, options are in a meaningful defined order (e.g. urban→rural
# gradient, educational progression) and should be displayed as-is. When false,
# the client should sort options alphabetically.
optionsOrdered: Boolean!
```

### Step 4 — Regenerate API code

```bash
cd api && make api-generate
```

This updates `graph/generated/generated.go` and `graph/model/models_gen.go`.

### Step 5 — API: resolver helper

**File**: `api/graph/resolver/search_fields_helpers.go`

Update both `case search.KindSingleEnum` and `case search.KindMultiEnum` blocks to
populate `OptionsOrdered` from `d.OptionsOrdered`:
```go
return &model.ChoiceSearchField{

    OptionsOrdered: d.OptionsOrdered,
    ...
}
```

### Step 6 — API: registry — mark ordered fields

**File**: `api/internal/search/registry.go`

Add `Ordered: true` to the 7 fields with meaningful ordering:

### Step 7 — Sync web schema


`optionsOrdered` field.

### Step 8 — Frontend: update GraphQL query

**File**: `web/src/pages/Search.tsx`

Add `optionsOrdered` to the `ChoiceSearchField` fragment in `SearchFieldsQuery`:
```graphql

  options {
    value
    displayName
  }
  optionsOrdered
  minSelections
  maxSelections
}
```

### Step 9 — Frontend: sort options in rendering

**File**: `web/src/components/search/FilterControl.tsx`

The `MultiSelectCheckboxes` component (line ~52) receives `field` as a prop.
Add a sort step before rendering:

```tsx

  ? field.options
  : [...field.options].sort((a, b) => a.displayName.localeCompare(b.displayName))
```

Apply the same sort for the single-select dropdown case (the `<select>` at line ~384).

Also pass `optionsOrdered` through `FilterControl` props to `MultiSelectCheckboxes` as needed.

### Step 10 — Tests

**API test** (`api/internal/search/search_test.go` or a new `field_test.go`):

- Verify that `SingleEnumField{Ordered: false}.Descriptor().OptionsOrdered == false`

**Frontend test** (`web/src/components/search/FilterControl.test.tsx`):
- Test that options render in alphabetical order when `optionsOrdered: false`
- Test that options preserve defined order when `optionsOrdered: true`

Facet sidebar checkboxes and dropdowns render options in registration order (the order
they appear in `registry.go`). This is inconsistent and arbitrary for most fields.
The fix: sort alphabetically by default on the frontend, but let the API signal
"preserve my defined order" for fields with a meaningful sequence (urban→rural,
educational progression, climate zones, etc.).

---

| `koppen_temperature` | SingleEnum | 9 | **Ordered** — Köppen sub-type scientific ordering |
| `koppen_precipitation` | SingleEnum | 3 | **Ordered** — scientific ordering |
| `state` | MultiColumnEnum | 50 | Alpha-sort (already alphabetical via `allStateOptions()`) |
| `institution_type` | SingleEnum | 3 | Alpha-sort |
| `coeducation_status` | GenderField | 3 | Alpha-sort |

content.text:
Plan submitted for approval. Here's a summary of what I've planned:

## Plan: Alpha-sort facet options

**The approach:** Add an `optionsOrdered: Boolean!` flag to the API's `ChoiceSearchField` GraphQL type. The frontend sorts alphabetically by default, and respects `optionsOrdered: true` to preserve the defined order.

**Enum facets inventory:**

| Field | Decision | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `locale` | **Ordered** | Urban→rural gradient |
| `region` | **Ordered** | Geographic grouping |
| `highest_degree` | **Ordered** | Certificate→Doctorate progression |
| `ncaa_division` | **Ordered** | Division I→II→III |
| `koppen_climate` | **Ordered** | Tropical→Polar scientific ordering |
| `koppen_temperature` | **Ordered** | Köppen sub-type scientific ordering |
| `koppen_precipitation` | **Ordered** | Scientific ordering |
| `state`, `institution_type`, `coeducation_status`, `application_platforms` | **Alpha-sort** | No meaningful sequence |

**Key changes:**
1. `FieldDescriptor` + 4 field structs: add `Ordered`/`OptionsOrdered bool`
2. GraphQL schema: add `optionsOrdered: Boolean!` to `ChoiceSearchField`
3. `registry.go`: mark 7 fields with `Ordered: true`
4. Frontend `FilterControl.tsx`: sort `[...options].sort(localeCompare)` when `optionsOrdered` is false
5. Tests for both API and frontend
Plan approved. Let me implement all the changes now.

type MultiEnumField struct {
	baseField
	Options       []EnumOption
	SelectionMode SelectionMode
	StorageFormat JSONBStorageFormat
	Ordered       bool
}

func (f *MultiEnumField) Descriptor() FieldDescriptor {
	d := f.baseField.Descriptor()
	d.Options = f.Options
	d.SelectionMode = f.SelectionMode
	d.OptionsOrdered = f.Ordered
	return d
}

}

func (f *MultiEnumField) Descriptor() FieldDescriptor {
	d := f.baseField.Descriptor()
	d.Options = f.Options
	d.SelectionMode = f.SelectionMode
	d.OptionsOrdered = f.Ordered
	return d
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
enum FieldVisibility {
  VISIBLE
  RESTRICTED
  HIDDEN
}

enum FieldSearchability {
  SEARCHABLE
  RESTRICTED
  NOT_SEARCHABLE
}

enum Theme {
  CORE
  LOCATION
  ACADEMICS
  COST
  OUTCOMES
  RANKING
  ADMISSIONS
  ATHLETICS
  FIELDS_OF_STUDY
  CULTURAL_ENVIRONMENT
  WEATHER
  PHYSICAL_ENVIRONMENT
  DEMOGRAPHICS
  REPUTATION
  SAFETY
  STUDENT_LIFE
  METADATA
  OTHER
}

# Shared fields on every search field type
interface SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# value is what is sent in filter payloads; displayName is shown to the user
type SearchFieldOption {
  value: String!
  displayName: String!
}

enum SearchFieldUnit {
  DOLLARS
  PERCENTAGE
  MILES
  HOURS
  MINUTES
}

# Full-text, substring, or exact-string text filter
type TextSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  minLength: Int
  maxLength: Int
  pattern: String
}

# Boolean toggle — checked sends value "1"; unchecked omits the filter entirely
type BinarySearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
}

# Single- or multi-select from a fixed option list
type ChoiceSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  options: [SearchFieldOption!]!
  minSelections: Int!
  maxSelections: Int
}

# Single exact numeric value
type NumericalSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  min: Float
  max: Float
  step: Float
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  defaultValue: Float
}

# Numeric lower–upper range pair
type NumericalRangeSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  min: Float
  max: Float
  step: Float
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  defaultLower: Float
  defaultUpper: Float
  minSpan: Float
  maxSpan: Float
}

# Geo proximity / bounding-box filter
type GeoSearchField implements SearchField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  searchability: FieldSearchability!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  defaultLatitude: Float
  defaultLongitude: Float
  maxRadiusMiles: Float!
}

type CipCode {
  code: String!
  description: String!
}

extend type Query {
  searchFields: [SearchField!]!
  searchOutputFields: [SearchOutputField!]!
  cipCodes(search: String!, limit: Int): [CipCode!]!
  cipCodesByCode(codes: [String!]!): [CipCode!]!
}

# Describes a field that appears in search result rows. Shares name/label/
# description/visibility/exportable with the corresponding SearchField where
# one exists; adds display-specific attributes (defaultVisible, sortable).
type SearchOutputField {
  name: String!
  label: String!
  description: String!
  theme: Theme!
  visibility: FieldVisibility!
  exportable: Boolean!
  dataSourceID: String
  # defaultVisible: true = shown by default in the results grid;
  # false = available in the column picker but hidden by default.
  defaultVisible: Boolean!
  # sortable: true = the column header shows a sort control.
  sortable: Boolean!
  # unit: optional hint for how to format the displayed value.
  unit: SearchFieldUnit
  # outputKey: dot-separated path into the data JSONB blob for extracting
  # the field's value from SearchInstitution.data. Null for fields that are
  # direct properties on SearchInstitution (name, city, state, etc.).
  outputKey: String
}

lines: +  # optionsOrdered: when true, options have a meaningful defined order (e.g. urban→rural
+  # gradient, educational progression, climate zones) and should be displayed as-is.
+  # When false, clients should sort options alphabetically by displayName.
